title: Will I Ever Be Normal Again After Kundalini Awakening?
meta_description: You're here, aren't you? It's probably late. The house is quiet, maybe too quiet. And you're scrolling, searching, desperate for an answer to that one ques
body:
Will I Ever Be Normal Again After Kundalini Awakening?

You're here, aren't you? It's probably late. The house is quiet, maybe too quiet. And you're scrolling, searching, desperate for an answer to that one question that's been gnawing at your insides: Will I ever be normal again? I know that question. I've asked it a million times, staring at the ceiling at 3 AM, feeling like my body was a foreign country and my mind a battlefield. You're not alone, not by a long shot. That feeling of being utterly broken, of having your reality ripped to shreds - yeah, I know it. I really do.

And let's get one thing straight right off the bat: "normal" is gone. It's not coming back. You might hate hearing that, and I get it. It sounds like a death sentence for the life you knew. But trust me, it's not. It's a birth announcement. You're not going back to who you were before this thing hit. And honestly? You wouldn't want to, even if you could. The person you were? They didn't know what you know now. They hadn't seen what you've seen. They hadn't felt what you've felt. You're different now. And that's okay. More than okay. It's what's supposed to happen.

I'm not going to sugarcoat this. What you're going through - the wild energy, the intense emotions, the feeling like you're losing your mind - it's rough. It's probably the hardest thing you'll ever face. I've been there. I've felt the terror, the despair, the absolute conviction that I was going insane and no one could help me. The truth is, a lot of what you're experiencing is a natural, albeit intense, part of this process. Your system is rebooting, clearing out decades of junk, and upgrading itself. That's not a gentle process. It's often violent, disorienting, and utterly exhausting. But here's the thing: you're not broken. You're being remade.

The "Crisis" Part of Kundalini Crisis

When you hear "kundalini awakening," people often picture bliss, enlightenment, floating on clouds. Right? But for a lot of us, it's anything but. It's a goddamn crisis. It's feeling like you're plugged into a faulty electrical socket, buzzing with energy that won't dissipate. It's physical symptoms that baffle doctors - tremors, burning, tingling, extreme heat or cold, muscle spasms, heart palpitations, weird digestive issues. It's emotional rollercoasters that make you question your sanity - intense fear, rage, grief, paranoia, feelings of cosmic insignificance, or conversely, immense power that feels too big for your body. I remember thinking I was having a heart attack almost daily, only for tests to come back clear. My nervous system was fried. I couldn't sleep, couldn't eat, couldn't think straight. It was pure hell.

You're probably experiencing some version of this. The sheer intensity can be terrifying because it feels so out of control. Your ego, your sense of self, is fighting for its life. It's being dismantled, and it doesn't like it one bit. This resistance creates a lot of the suffering. You're trying to cling to the old "normal," but the universe has other plans for you. It's like trying to hold onto a sandcastle while a tsunami hits. It's futile, and it just makes the ride bumpier. You're not going crazy; you're going through a deep transformation. It just feels like insanity because your old self is dying. And that's a painful process, both physically and psychologically.

I'll tell you what really helped me understand what the hell was happening. Books. Not just any books, but ones that actually talked about the raw, gritty reality of this. The Kundalini Experience by Lee Sannella (paid link) was a lifesaver. It showed me I wasn't alone, that others had gone through the same terrifying symptoms. It gave me a approach, a language, for what felt utterly inexpressible. And When Spirit Leaps: Navigating the Process of Spiritual Awakening by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) also helped me feel less isolated, less like a freak. These aren't easy reads, necessarily, but they're honest. They don't pretend it's all rainbows and unicorns. They talk about the dark nights of the soul, the disorientation, the sheer terror. Know what I mean? It's important to know you're not the only one feeling this way.

What's Happening To You - The Mechanics

So, what exactly is going on? Look, it's not some woo-woo thing that only happens to yogis in caves. This is a real energetic phenomenon. Imagine your body is a complex electrical system. For most of your life, only a fraction of its capacity is active. Kundalini awakening is like flipping a master switch, sending a surge of high-voltage energy through circuits that aren't quite ready for it. This energy, often called Kundalini shakti, starts moving, cleaning, purifying. It hits blockages - physical, emotional, mental - and it blasts through them. That's where the "crisis" comes in.

  • Physical Purging: The energy is literally cleaning out your system. Old traumas stored in your cells, toxins, suppressed emotions - it's all getting stirred up and released. This can manifest as those weird physical symptoms: pain, heat, cold, vibrations, involuntary movements. Your body is detoxing on a deep, deep level.
  • Emotional Release: Get ready for the feels. Old grief, anger, fear, shame - stuff you thought you'd dealt with years ago, or maybe didn't even know was there - it's coming to the surface. It can feel overwhelming, like you're drowning in emotions. You might cry uncontrollably, feel rage for no apparent reason, or be gripped by intense anxiety. This is the energy clearing out your emotional body.
  • Mental Deconstruction: Your beliefs, your worldview, your sense of identity - it's all being questioned and often shattered. This can lead to periods of extreme confusion, existential dread, loss of meaning, and even temporary psychosis-like states where reality feels very fluid. Your mind is being rewired.
  • Spiritual Expansion: Amidst all the chaos, there can be moments of deep clarity, deep peace, connection to something vast and loving, or flashes of insight. These glimpses are often what keep you going, reminding you there's a purpose to all this madness. You're connecting to a deeper part of yourself, your true nature.

This isn't a linear process. It's not like you go through phase one, then phase two. It's often cyclical, messy, and unpredictable. You'll have good days, bad days, and days where you don't know what day it is. But through it all, something new is being built. A stronger, more authentic you.

Finding Your Ground

So, what do you do when everything feels like it's falling apart? You find your ground. You build new anchors. Because the old ones - your job, your relationships, your hobbies, your identity - they might not feel stable anymore. Here's what I learned, often the hard way:

  • Radical Acceptance: This is huge. Stop fighting it. Stop wishing it would go away. It won't. The more you resist, the more it hurts. Accept that this is your reality right now. "Okay, this is happening. I don't understand it, I don't like it, but I accept it." That simple shift can bring immense relief.
  • Self-Care is Non-Negotiable: This isn't a luxury; it's survival. Sleep when you can. Eat nourishing food, even if you don't feel like it. Hydrate. Spend time in nature. Gentle movement - walking, stretching, slow yoga - can help move the energy without overstimulating you. You're recovering from a spiritual heart attack, basically. Treat yourself like you're in intensive care.
  • Grounding Practices: You need to bring that wild energy down. Earth yourself. Walk barefoot on grass or dirt. Visualize roots growing from your feet into the earth. Eat root vegetables. Take warm baths with Epsom salts. Spend time near trees. These aren't cures, but they help manage the intensity.
  • Find Support: This is probably the hardest part. Most people won't get it. Your friends and family might think you're having a nervous breakdown. That's why finding people who understand is so important. Look for online communities, spiritual emergence groups, or a therapist who specializes in spiritual crises. You need someone to talk to who won't tell you to just "snap out of it."
  • Limit Stimulation: Too much input - loud noises, bright lights, chaotic environments, too much screen time, caffeine, sugar - can exacerbate symptoms. Simplify your life as much as possible. Create a calm sanctuary for yourself.
  • Journaling: Get it out. All of it. The fears, the visions, the confusion, the pain. Don't censor yourself. Just write. It helps to externalize some of the internal chaos.

Another book that helped me make sense of the bigger picture, especially the spiritual side of things, was The Kundalini Guide by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link). It's more of a roadmap for understanding the process itself, which can be incredibly reassuring when you feel completely lost. It's not about fixing it, but about understanding it, which is the first step towards managing it.

The New "Normal" - And Why It's Better

So, you're not going back to your old normal. But what's the new normal like? Honestly, it's pretty incredible. It doesn't mean you'll never have struggles again, or that life will be perfect. But you'll be different. You'll be more real, more connected, more resilient. Here's what I've found on the other side of the crisis:

  • Increased Sensitivity: You'll be more aware - of energy, of emotions (yours and others), of subtle shifts in your environment. This can be challenging at first, but it also means you'll experience beauty and connection more deeply.
  • Greater Authenticity: You won't be able to tolerate inauthenticity - in yourself or others. You'll shed friendships, jobs, and situations that don't align with your true self. This can be painful, but it frees you up to live a life that's genuinely yours.
  • Deeper Intuition: Your inner knowing will be much stronger. You'll trust your gut more. Decisions will feel clearer.
  • More Presence: You'll be more in the moment, less caught up in past regrets or future anxieties. Life will feel richer, more vibrant.
  • deep Compassion: Having gone through such a challenging process, you'll have a deeper understanding and empathy for others' suffering.
  • A Sense of Purpose: You might discover a new calling, a deeper meaning to your life that wasn't there before. The universe put you through this for a reason, and often, that reason involves serving others in some way.

Does that land? It's not about going back; it's about moving forward into a much bigger version of yourself. It’s about becoming who you were always meant to be, stripped of all the societal conditioning and old wounds. It's not easy getting there, but the destination is worth the journey. You're not just surviving; you're evolving.

Managing the Long Haul - It's a Marathon, Not a Sprint

This isn't a quick fix. This isn't a 30-day program. This is your life now. The intensity might ebb and flow, but the changes are permanent. So yeah, you need to learn how to manage this new reality for the long haul. It means developing new habits, new ways of relating to yourself and the world.

I can't stress enough the importance of patience. You didn't get into this state overnight, and you won't "recover" overnight. Recovery isn't about going back to normal; it's about integrating this deep experience into your being. It's about learning to live with this heightened state, to master it, rather than be overwhelmed by it.

You'll have setbacks. There will be days when you feel like you've made no progress, or even gone backward. That's okay. It's part of the process. Don't beat yourself up. Just acknowledge it, do what you need to do to care for yourself, and keep moving forward, even if it's just one tiny step at a time. I've had many moments where I felt like I was back at square one, but then I'd realize I was actually handling it better, with more awareness, than I would have before. That's progress.

Remember that the mind can be a powerful enemy during this time. It will try to convince you that you're broken, that you're dying, that there's no hope. That's why it's so important to have practices that help you quiet the mind, even for a few minutes. Meditation, even just focusing on your breath, can be incredibly helpful - but be careful not to overdo it, as some meditation practices can actually intensify kundalini. Focus on gentle, grounding techniques rather than intense energy work.

Sometimes, the mental anguish can feel like a bottomless pit. I remember reading Darkness Visible: A Memoir of Madness by William Styron (paid link) during one of my darkest periods. While it's about clinical depression, not kundalini, it articulated the sheer, crushing weight of mental suffering in a way that made me feel seen. It reminded me that even in the deepest despair, there's a human connection to be found in shared experience. You're not the first to feel this level of internal torment, and you won't be the last. And just like Styron found his way back, you will too.

So, will you ever be normal again? No. You'll be something far more unusual. You're becoming who you were always meant to be. It's a rough road, I won't lie. But you've got this. You're stronger than you know. And you're not alone. Keep going. One breath, one moment, one day at a time. The light is there, waiting for you to fully step into it.

This is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you're in crisis, please reach out to a qualified therapist or call 988.
author: Kalesh
image_alt: Will I Ever Be Normal Again After Kundalini Awakening?
